reasoning: The question asks whether AT&T (T) will have a higher close price on 2026-01-24 compared to its close price on 2025-10-26, a horizon of approximately three months. Over a generic three-month stock market horizon, the probability of an individual stock generating a positive price return is generally around 52-55% due to the upward equity drift. As a mature, high-dividend stock, AT&T generates a significant portion of its total return through quarterly dividends. A three-month period from late October to late January includes typically one ex-dividend date (early January), which drops the stock price by roughly 1%. Assuming a risk-weighted expected total equity return over three months of about 2%, Subtracting the 1% dividend yields an expected price appreciation of ~1%. Given AT&T's three-month historically implied volatility of around 10-12%, a 1% expected positive drift results in a z-score of roughly 0.1, corresponding to a ~53%-54% probability that the price ends strictly higher. Therefore, I estimate a 53% probability.
